2605.10828.title: The First Drop of Ink: Nonlinear Impact of Misleading Information in Long-Context Reasoning
2605.10828.paper_summary: As large language models are increasingly deployed in retrieval-augmented generation and agentic systems that accumulate extensive context, understanding how distracting information affects long-context performance becomes critical. Prior work shows that semantically relevant yet misleading documents degrade performance, but the quantitative relationship between the proportion of distractors and performance remains unstudied. In this work, we systematically vary the hard-distractor proportion in fixed-length contexts, revealing a striking nonlinear pattern: as the proportion of hard distractors increases, performance drops sharply within the first small fraction, while the remainder of the range yields only marginal additional decline. We term this ''The First Drop of Ink'' effect, analogous to how a single drop of ink contaminates water. Our theoretical and empirical analyses grounded in attention mechanics show that hard distractors capture disproportionate attention even at small proportions, with diminishing marginal impact as their proportion grows. Controlled experiments further show that filtering gains mainly come from context-length reduction rather than distractor removal; substantial recovery requires reducing the hard-distractor proportion to near zero, highlighting the importance of upstream retrieval precision.
2605.10828.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型在增強檢索生成和累積大量上下文的代理系統中越來越多地被部署，理解干擾信息如何影響長上下文性能變得至關重要。以往的研究顯示，語義上相關但具有誤導性的文檔會降低性能，但干擾者比例與性能之間的定量關係仍未被研究。在本研究中，我們系統性地變化固定長度上下文中的難干擾者比例，揭示了一個引人注目的非線性模式：隨著難干擾者比例的增加，性能在最初的一小部分內急劇下降，而其餘範圍僅產生邊際的額外下降。我們將這稱為「墨水的第一次下降」效應，類似於一滴墨水如何污染水。我們基於注意力機制的理論和實證分析顯示，即使在小比例下，難干擾者也會捕獲不成比例的注意力，隨著其比例的增長，邊際影響逐漸減少。受控實驗進一步顯示，過濾的收益主要來自於上下文長度的減少，而非干擾者的移除；要實現實質性的恢復，需要將難干擾者比例降低到接近零，這突顯了上游檢索精度的重要性。
2605.10828.author: Muhan Gao et.al.
2605.10828.authors: Muhan Gao, Zih-Ching Chen, Kuan-Hao Huang
